title: fix(audio): complete Stable Audio 3 metadata
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds `stable-audio-3` as an alias for the direct Stability-backed Large model.
- Advertises audio input for both Stable Audio 3 variants, matching their existing `reference_audio` support.
- Extends model-list assertions and relies on the shared alias matrix for resolution coverage.

Tests:
- `npx vitest run test/aliases.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (239 passed)
- `npx vitest run test/index.test.ts --testNamePattern='stable-audio-3-(medium|large)' --reporter=verbose` (6 passed)

Addresses #11212; the LoRA workflow remains out of scope.

gist.headline: Audio metadata found its wings
gist.blurb: Stable Audio 3’s labels have been tidied up: the direct Stability Large model now answers to `stable-audio-3`, and both variants openly accept reference audio. The hive’s model map is slightly less mysterious.
gist.summary: Completed Stable Audio 3 registry metadata by adding `stable-audio-3` as an alias for the direct Stability-backed Large model and declaring audio input support for both `stable-audio-3-medium` and `stable-audio-3-large`.
gist.impact: Developers can use the simpler `stable-audio-3` model name and will see reference-audio capability correctly advertised for both Stable Audio 3 variants.
gist.visual_concept: Depict a cozy audio garden where Polly helps a pair of Stable Audio 3 music machines identify their matching sound inputs: one Large, one Medium, connected by clear labeled cables and a small reference-audio cassette. The visual metaphor is corrected model metadata becoming a tidy, usable patch bay, with flowering waveform vines and a CRT robot checking the labels.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, a cute yellow-and-dark-brown striped bee with pink cheek blush, light blue wings, and tiny studio headphones, carefully plugging a reference-audio cassette into two friendly retro music terminals labeled “SA3 MEDIUM” and “SA3 LARGE”; a CRT monitor robot happily verifies a little alias tag reading “stable-audio-3.” Lime green (#ecf874) patch cables and waveform flowers grow through a warm cream, mint, lavender, and peach garden workshop; cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, subtle CRT glow and scanlines.
